name: focus_adhd_pink
description: ADHD-optimized: Beta AM (18 Hz) bed + evidence-backed pink noise.
references: Söderlund, G., Sikström, S., & Smart, A. (2007). Listen to the noise: noise is beneficial for cognitive performance in ADHD.
Helps, S.K., et al. (2014). Different noise types and ADHD symptoms.
Stochastic resonance literature on optimal noise for neural processing.
rationale: CIM ADHD focus: slightly higher bed mix (-12 dB) and AM depth (0.08) to account for potentially lower entrainment sensitivity. Pink noise chosen as evidence-first default (strongest literature support for cognitive improvement in ADHD). ADHD EQ profile mid-scoops noise to reduce masking of music/speech. No noise AM — avoids creating a "modulated noise layer" that separates from the music.
